items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Picton Castle
*Country:* wales
*Region:* west wales
*County:* Pembrokeshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 51.784, -4.885
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Picton_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Picton%20Castle/@51.7833,-4.8667,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZMMefXJYknofWMQqzyqj17sLFfB_emfwPiBCrVsDMCxhR6RWE3fEyWMtfKN68aC-FQHo6NMkckiZ8ZBzSDj8_IDgEUaVVSpCc8_JGXSnErXLcp8YmtvQkqPnpLAV4c0k2gs2GUtzeey9pWprHa_QZbPcA=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* change google places link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/jgFsoopMCjmu74GMA
add source - https://pictoncastle.co.uk/

*Description:*
Picton Castle is a medieval castle near Haverfordwest in the community of Uzmaston, Boulston and Slebech, Pembrokeshire, Wales. Originally built at the end of the 13th century by a Flemish knight, it later came into the hands of Sir John Wogan. The castle and gardens are now owned and managed by the Picton Castle Trust, a registered charity, for the benefit of the public. It is of unusual construction and has been remodelled several times during its history. The castle is a Grade I listed building and its gardens and park are designated at Grade II* on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

_Listing 2 of 94_

*Description:*
Aberglasney House and Gardens is a medieval house and gardens set in the Tywi valley in the parish of Llangathen, Carmarthenshire, West Wales. It is owned and run by Aberglasney Restoration Trust, a registered charity. The house is a Grade II* listed building and the gardens are listed, also at Grade II*, on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

_Listing 3 of 94_

listing.desc: Aberglasney House and Gardens is a medieval house and gardens set in the Tywi valley in the parish of Llangathen, Carmarthenshire, West Wales. The house is a Grade II* listed building and the gardens are listed, also at Grade II*, on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

imageAttribution.description: Aberglasney House Viewed from the now restored Cloister Garden, the exterior of the house now looks in good shape. Inside, there is still a lot of restoration work going on.

*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Lamphey%20Bishop's%20Palace/@51.668,-4.858,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZN5DaeQQjYnmGUmEPeU79uZT6pt5KkKe8AwETxppk03Xl3ZoUOw02MSHkyscV-AUkrItQ4JE3cy-Rs4V69rebUY0ON8AKt5-vV5clSVdM7jhkwzSXbFSXxCZvF1ZnX9J-dOKZ4S8hGq6v8kdA=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Lamphey Bishop's Palace or Lamphey Palace is a ruined medieval building complex in Lamphey, Pembrokeshire. It is a scheduled ancient monument and a Grade I Listed building. The grounds of the palace are designated, jointly with the park of Lamphey Court, as Grade II* on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

_Listing 4 of 94_

The grounds of the palace are designated, jointly with the park of Lamphey Court, as Grade II* on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Paxton's Tower
*Country:* wales
*Region:* west wales
*County:* Carmarthenshire
*Type:* monument
*Period:* 19th century
*Coords:* 51.85183, -4.1198
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paxton's_Tower
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Paxton's%20Tower/@51.8528,-4.0361,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZMz0DYN3G5LAOaser9RajpKl1WBEWbeFUwQFXSL0LTsxdaSdqPO5jtI_xTbhcINvRE-IuJuUU-lkHAZV4ht2pU2K9dHC-So4-d5eTDKWkCV5MY8NiaB177VZ6zAUwPZHWdIcXyC5V6a6CYX2A=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Paxton's Tower is a Neo-Gothic folly erected in honour of Lord Nelson. It is situated on the top of a hill near Llanarthney in the River Tywi valley in Carmarthenshire, Wales. It is a visitor attraction that can be combined with a visit to the nearby National Botanic Garden of Wales. Its high location provides views over the Botanic Gardens and the Tywi valley. The tower, a Grade II* listed building, is under the care of the National Trust. The surrounding parkland is registered at Grade II* on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

_Listing 5 of 94_

listing.name: Paxton's Tower
listing.desc: Paxton's Tower is a Neo-Gothic folly erected in honour of Lord Nelson. It is situated on the top of a hill near Llanarthney in the River Tywi valley in Carmarthenshire, Wales. It is a visitor attraction that can be combined with a visit to the nearby National Botanic Garden of Wales. Its high location provides views over the Botanic Gardens and the Tywi valley. The tower, a Grade II* listed building, is under the care of the National Trust. The surrounding parkland is registered at Grade II* on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Dynevor Castle
*Country:* wales
*Region:* west wales
*County:* Carmarthenshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 12th-13th century
*Coords:* 51.8768, -4.0184
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dinefwr_Castle
*Maps:* https://maps.app.goo.gl/nPsHYLLgEkHaJ8HX6
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Dinefwr_castle_at_sunrise.jpg/330px-Dinefwr_castle_at_sunrise.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Dinefwr Castle is a ruined castle overlooking the River Towy near the town of Llandeilo, Carmarthenshire, Wales. It lies on a ridge on the northern bank of the Towy, with a steep drop of one hundred feet to the river. Dinefwr was the chief seat of the Dinefwr dynasty of the Kingdom of Deheubarth. The castle is a Grade I listed building.

_Listing 6 of 94_

listing.desc: Dinefwr Castle is a ruined castle overlooking the River Towy near the town of Llandeilo, Carmarthenshire, Wales. The castle is a Grade I listed building.

*Name:* Dalkey Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* dublin and the pale
*County:* County Dublin
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 15th century
*Coords:* 53.2779, -6.1055
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dalkey_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Dalkey%20Castle/@53.2722,-6.1,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZPIpyzhEmJ-cn0IuR_N4lhOvd-idVM-iGQTvOzl9Lkb1f3znVvonAwPiFa2BJnCMPgPxUYK-o42K2OCrikcC3bXOmRv-SBwatki25WdB93OQdH8xcKF7efAeBKW2WxNi6Zf2RIYkwLlR32JnA=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source- https://dalkeycastle.com/

*Description:*
Dalkey Castle, formerly known as Goat Castle, is a medieval structure in Castle Street, Dalkey, Dublin, Ireland. The complex currently accommodates the Dalkey Heritage Centre, which is in the castle itself, and Dalkey Town Hall, which is formed by a single storey extension behind the original building.

_Listing 7 of 94_
